subject: A pale, stern-looking woman with short, swept-up blonde hair stands at the center of a violent burst of energy. Her arms are outstretched as cracks of light and dark erupt outward from her body, suggesting a destructive spell being cast. She wears dark, formal-looking attire with a high collar.
description: A grim, finely-dressed woman casts a destructive spell at the viewer. Her arms are flung wide and her body is the epicenter of a radiating shockwave of cracked light and shadow that fractures the space around her. The palette is dominated by sepia, ash-gray, and pale gold, with her skin and hair catching the brightest light. Her expression is cold and resolute — a magistrate or aristocrat passing judgment rather than a wild caster. The composition is a centered mid-shot with strong radial symmetry, and the energy effect reads as both holy and necrotic, fitting the Orzhov color identity.
foreground: robed woman with arms outstretched, casting a radial burst of cracked white-and-black energy
background: fractured ambient haze, broken light, indeterminate space radiating outward from the figure
emotion: cold judgment, righteous condemnation, austere fury
